task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL application window is open with the 2HBS structure loaded, visible in the right-hand object menu or the command history.

task_completion.description: Modify B-factors and save the new structure to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal, file manager, or PyMOL command history showing the creation/saving of '2HBS_hydrophobic.pdb' in the '~/PyMOL_Data/structures/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a surface representation colored by the new binary B-factors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL 3D viewer displays the protein as a solid surface, colored in a distinct two-tone/binary color scheme reflecting the 50.0 (hydrophobic) and 10.0 (polar/charged) B-factor assignment.

task_completion.description: Identify and display the Valine 6 mutations on the beta chains as spheres.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL 3D viewer shows specific residues (Val6) rendered as spheres, distinct from the main surface representation.

task_completion.description: Render and save a PNG figure of the surface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal output, or image viewer showing 'hbs_surface.png' located in '~/PyMOL_Data/images/'.

task_completion.description: Write a report identifying the mutated chains, mutation, key residues, and measured distance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., using 'cat') displaying the contents of '~/PyMOL_Data/hbs_report.txt'. The text must mention Chains B and D, Valine 6, Phe 85, Leu 88, and a distance value (around 24.4 Angstroms).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used PyMOL or a PyMOL Python script to perform the structural modifications and rendering.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the PyMOL GUI, PyMOL command-line interface, or a Python script importing 'pymol' being executed, rather than the agent just downloading a pre-existing image.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually measured the distance rather than hallucinating a number.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a measurement object (dashed line with a number) in the PyMOL viewer, a 'distance' command in the PyMOL console, or a script calculating the distance between the specific C-alpha atoms.

integrity.description: Verify the output files are genuinely created in the correct directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (like 'ls', 'cat', 'mkdir') or a file explorer confirming the paths '~/PyMOL_Data/structures/', '~/PyMOL_Data/images/', and '~/PyMOL_Data/' contain the respective files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected PDB ID is 2HBS. The modified PDB must be saved to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/structures/2HBS_hydrophobic.pdb'. The rendered image must be saved to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/hbs_surface.png' and be at least 30,000 bytes. The report must be saved to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/hbs_report.txt'. The report must correctly identify Chains B and D, Valine 6, Phe 85, Leu 88, and report an intramolecular distance between 15.0 and 35.0 Angstroms (the exact empirical distance is ~24.42 Angstroms).
